title: Mermaid Cave from Above | Oahu Drone View
creator: Elevate the View (@elevatetheview)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the sea-cave / natural-rock-arch coastal-landscape subgenre bucket, which this pack did not previously contain (independently confirmed absent from the pre-existing 31-file exemplar directory before writing this record: no Oahu/Mermaid-Cave entry present). A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/eqYvPMqgd80); per this task's step-1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via `curl "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/eqYvPMqgd80&format=json"`, which returned title="Mermaid Cave from Above | Oahu Drone View", author_name="Elevate the View", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@elevatetheview", provider_name="YouTube", height=200, width=113 -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims, and the 113x200 portrait aspect ratio independently confirms the vertical/Shorts framing the candidate's why_short_form_reel_style claim rested on. This session additionally fetched the canonical watch-page form (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eqYvPMqgd80) via curl with a browser user-agent, specifically to check for a Creative Commons marking; the response (200 OK, 82 lines) was a minimal script/consent-stub page (YouTube's client-side-rendered shell, not the fully hydrated watch page) containing no "Creative Commons", "CC BY", or "licenseType" string anywhere -- consistent with, though not identical in mechanism to, the candidate's description of the full page being blocked (this session observed a stub/consent-gated response rather than a literal CAPTCHA challenge specifically, so that distinction is noted rather than repeated uncritically). This session went further than the candidate's own verification by also fetching the channel's About page (https://www.youtube.com/@elevatetheview/about, 200 OK), which returned subscriberCountText="50 subscribers", viewCountText="122,702 views", and joinedDateText="Joined May 12, 2025" -- i.e. a small, very recently created channel with no independently corroborated professional/brand affiliation (no DJI/brand credit, no press coverage, no large following found), which is used below purely as a scoring-calibration signal (this is a hobbyist/small-creator upload, not a professionally-branded or contest-verified one) -- it is not used to alter award_or_showcase_provenance, which stays null since no juried/broadcaster placement of this specific video was found. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking was found in this session's own fetch of the watch-page stub, and per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file or frame was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response for Shorts does not include a duration field, and the watch-page fetch returned only the unhydrated stub (no player-response JSON), so no runtime could be read this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number (Shorts are conventionally <=60s, but that is a genre convention, not a measurement), this field is left unset pending a real YouTube Data API call or manual playback confirmation. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.88 range: sharpness=0.72 -- placed at this pack's un-curated-personal-account floor (matching flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.72 and below fishaholic-tt-tobago-lagoon-coastal.json's 0.75/matthiasdangl's 0.79) because this session found no independently corroborated professional gear, brand affiliation, or resolution/bitrate claim for this specific upload -- only a 50-subscriber, 2025-joined channel -- so no basis exists to score above this pack's baseline for unverified small-creator content. exposure=0.71 -- scored slightly below the sharpness estimate and below this pack's mid-tier tropical-daylight cases (matthiasdangl's 0.76) because a sea-cave/rock-arch subject inherently combines bright open-ocean/sky exposure with a darker cave interior in the same frame, a genuinely harder high-dynamic-range exposure condition than an open-water or open-landscape shot, and no HDR/log profile claim was found for this upload to offset that difficulty; still placed above this pack's hardest-documented exposure case (lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.70 backlit sunset wave-face) since the title/thumbnail framing suggests a mid-day reveal rather than a dusk/backlit shot specifically. motion_smoothness=0.75 -- scored at this pack's lower-middle tier (matching flaviufpv's 0.74, below matthiasdangl's 0.85) because this is a single-location 'reveal' shot per its title, implying a shorter, likely slower hover-and-reveal camera move typical of consumer/prosumer camera-drone content rather than a confirmed sustained FPV one-shot -- no editorial/live-playback review was conducted this session (see editorial_style below) to confirm continuity or cut count, so this estimate is not elevated on an unconfirmed single-take claim the way matthiasdangl's record was, where a live-playback session had directly confirmed unbroken seekability. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.71, motion_smoothness=0.75, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session; `default_weights()` resolved to weights_version='default-v2' (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.72+0.71+0.75)/3 = 0.7266666666666666, confirmed by actually running the function this session (not hand-computed), rounded to 0.7267 here. `editorial_style` is deliberately left at `review_method: 'not_reviewed'` per this task's supplied editorial-review data: no live-playback or text-provenance editorial review was conducted for this record in this session, so `format`, `estimated_cut_count`, `avg_shot_length_seconds`, and `pacing_notes` are all left null and `transition_styles_observed` left empty rather than inferred or guessed, per this schema's honesty contract.
